entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop affected by grain mold disease worldwide.

entities.name: GRAIN MOLD
entities.description: A widespread disease causing preharvest grain deterioration in sorghum.

entities.name: FUNGAL SPECIES
entities.description: Various fungi that interact parasitically or saprophytically to cause grain mold.

entities.name: FUSARIUM SPECIES
entities.description: Major fungal pathogens of worldwide significance involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: CURVULARIA LUNATA
entities.description: A major fungal pathogen associated with grain mold.

entities.name: ALTERNARIA ALTERNATA
entities.description: A major fungal pathogen associated with grain mold.

entities.name: FUSARIUM ANDIYAGI
entities.description: A species of Fusarium involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: FUSARIUM PROLIFERATUM
entities.description: A species of Fusarium involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: FUSARIUM THAPSINUM
entities.description: A species of Fusarium involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: E SACCHARI
entities.description: A species involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: E VERTICILLIOIDES
entities.description: A species involved in the grain mold complex.

entities.name: MYCOTOXINS
entities.description: Harmful toxins produced by certain Fusarium species.

entities.name: FUMONISINS
entities.description: A type of mycotoxin produced by Fusarium species.

entities.name: MONILIFORMIS TRICHOTHECENES
entities.description: A type of mycotoxin produced by Fusarium species.

entities.name: FUSAPROLIFERIN
entities.description: A type of mycotoxin produced by Fusarium species.

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: The quality and marketability of grains as food or feed sources.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period during which short- and medium-duration sorghum cultivars mature, increasing disease risk.

entities.name: HUMID TROPICAL AND SUBTROPICAL CLIMATES
entities.description: Climates where grain mold is particularly important.
